Example: Prosim, če mi daste konkreten odgovor na konkretno vprašanje, kajti enačenje in posploševanje vaših odgovorov,[...]
Example (Translation): Please give me a specific answer to a specific question, because equating and generalizing your answers, [...]
Notes/Rationale: The collocation with the highest logDice, which does not exceed 10, suggesting that while the association between “vaš” and “odgovor” is strong, it is not a formulaic or fixed phrase. It is often used to express the typical polite phrase “Hvala za vaš odgovor”, though not exclusively, and in addition to prompting a speaker to give an answer, it can also function as an indirect form of attack.

Example: Hvala za vašo pozornost.
Example (Translation): Thank you for your attention.
Notes/Rationale: Similar function as odgovor, though here the collocate is almost exclusively a polite phrase (Hvala za vašo pozornost/Thank you for your attention).

Example: Vaše ministrstvo je kapitalno pogrnilo na referendumu o vodah.
Example (Translation): Your ministry suffered a major defeat in the referendum on water.
Notes/Rationale: Your Ministry: the collocation serves as a direct form of attack to criticise the work of the Ministry (and, by extension, a (coalition) party, speaker, etc.), which can be positioned as a formal (political) authority.

Example: Nekredibilno, popolnoma nekredibilno z vaše strani.
Example (Translation): “Not credible—absolutely not credible on your part.”
Notes/Rationale: Vaša stran (Your side): Can refer directly to a particular party/parliamentary group (from the opposing side), ideological positioning (e.g. Na vaši strani so pa nekateri taki, ki jim samostojnost in neodvisnost Slovenije nista intimna opcija. /On your side, there are some who do not consider Slovenia's independence and autonomy to be an intimate option. ) or individual speaker (Spoštovani gospod Vrtovec, ko je bilo tudi iz vaše strani rečeno, da [...])

Example: Takih informacij, veste, jaz slišim vsak dan sto, torej bi po vašem mnenju moral vsak dan napisati sto kazenskih prijav.
Example (Translation): You know, I hear this kind of information every day, so in your opinion, I should file a hundred criminal complaints every day.
Notes/Rationale: Vaše mnenje (Your opinion): Questions, relating to the opinion of the MP/Minister (Spoštovani minister, zato me zanima vaše mnenje: […]) during MP questions, or a political strategy to delegitimize and frame the opposing side viewpoint as subjective (as seen in the example).

Example: Skratka, vaša izjava je še en mozaik k temu vašemu dojemanju, ki ga očitno delite s svojo stranko SDS in s predsednikom Vlade o sodstvu.
Example (Translation): In short, your statement is yet another piece of the mosaic that makes up your perception, which you obviously share with your party, the SDS, and with the Prime Minister regarding the judiciary.
Notes/Rationale: Vaša izjava (Your statement): Discourse activity, either referring to a particular MP, Minister, or a specific (ideologically) opposing group (party, coalition, or opposition)

Example: Tega pa ne moremo reči za tiste medije, ki so blizu vaše stranke.
Example (Translation): However, we cannot say the same for those media outlets that are close to your party.
Notes/Rationale: Vaša stranka (Your party): Explicit reference to a political party; often serves as object of blame/attack.

Example: No, in zdaj taka skrb, ki ste jo pokazali tukaj s temi Murglami in s temi vašimi predhodniki, vašimi vzorniki, partijskimi veljaki, ki so za tako nizke cene dobili stanovanja, [...]
Example (Translation): Well, and now this concern that you have shown here with these Murglas and your predecessors, your role models, party bigwigs who got apartments for such low prices[...]
Notes/Rationale: Vaš predhodnik (Your predecessor): A (political) predecessor, relates to the role (and function) of an MP/party or a speaker.

Example: Vi si itak mislite, saj stvari bodo šle čez, imamo dovolj glasov. Vaši koalicijski poslanci zadeve podprejo, ne glede na vse
Example (Translation): You think that things will go through anyway, that we have enough votes. Your coalition MPs will support the matter, no matter what.
Notes/Rationale: Your coalition (colleague/government/partner/...): An adjective that describes the explicit relationship of the subject to a coalition. It implies that the subject is part of the coalition, refers to shared political allies, and often suggests political advantages granted to the opposing side by this political ally or authority.

Example: In s kršenjem pravil boste pripeljali politiko čez vrata naših šol. Zavržno in zelo pomenljivo za konec vašega mandata.
Example (Translation): And by breaking the rules, you will bring politics into our schools. This is reprehensible and very significant for the end of your term in office.
Notes/Rationale: Vaš mandat (Your mandate/term): Use of parliamentary mandate to denote the problems/broken promises in the current/previous mandate of the opposing side (i.e. Their period in power).

Example: Ravno tako dobro vemo, da za podporo tej vaši vladi, ki se s korupcijo kar rada ukvarja, se lahko legalizira tudi črna gradnja na atraktivni lokaciji, ki je bila prej lopa, recimo ob morju.
Example (Translation): We also know very well that in order to support your government, which is quite fond of corruption, illegal construction in attractive locations, such as by the sea, can be legalized.
Notes/Rationale: Vaša vlada (Your Government): Contrary to our Government, here there is no duality in the meaning. It is used to point out the blame and attack of (current/previous) government.

Example: Kasneje se je seveda izkazalo, da tiste vaše trditve niso najbolj stale. Pa kljub temu. Stanje danes imamo, da imamo še zmeraj ogromno težav [...]
Example (Translation): Later, of course, it turned out that your claims were not entirely accurate. Nevertheless, the situation today is that we still have enormous problems.
Notes/Rationale: Vaša trditev (Your claim): Similar function to vaša izjava.

Example: Sam se tudi zavedam, da z mojo razpravo ne bom spremenil vaših odločitev, bi pa nekaj stvari ponovil, [...]
Example (Translation): I am also aware that my discussion will not change your decisions, but I would like to reiterate a few things.
Notes/Rationale: Vaša odločitev (Your decision): Parliamentary activity; used to frame responsibility, caution, or criticism regarding decisions made by the opposing side.

Example: In ne zavajajte, da so vse rejniške pokrite z vašim predlogom zakona, ker vam je vaš državni sekretar povedal jasno in glasno, da niso.
Example (Translation): And don't mislead people into thinking that all foster parents are covered by your proposed legislation, because your state secretary has told you loud and clear that they are not.
Notes/Rationale: Vaš predlog (Your proposal): Similar function to odločitev.

Example: Od začetka epidemije in vašega nastopa funkcije ministrice sem na vas naslovila več vprašanj [...]
Example (Translation): Since the beginning of the epidemic and your appointment as minister, I have addressed several questions to you [...]
Notes/Rationale: Vaš nastop (Your appointment (to a role)/appearance/performance): Collocations with several different meanings - performance, speech, or behaviour in parliamentary debate, media,  public appearances of a speaker, as well as their appointment to a role.

Example: Ampak za razliko od vašega stališča, jaz sem prebrala vašo novelo zakona.
Example (Translation): But unlike you, I have read your draft amendment.
Notes/Rationale: Vaše stališče (Your position): Similar function to vaše mnenje. Also very prominent in MP questions segments (mostly function as pointed questions to MP of the opposing side or , when directed at MPs from the same party or coalition, serving as an opportunity for them to respond by highlighting the effectiveness of their own work (or that of the government, party, etc.).

Example: Pa ne govorim to konkretno za vas, kolega Prebil, ampak na splošno za vašo politično opcijo.
Example (Translation): I am not referring specifically to you, Mr. Prebil, but generally to your political option.
Notes/Rationale: Your political (responsibility/agenda/colleague/allies/party/...): Adjective, similar in function to Your coalition. However, in this case, the explicit reference to the group to which the speaker belongs is not clear.

Example: Dokažite! Vse ostalo vam škodi in če bo škodilo zdaj, vam bo škodilo kasneje, ker to je karma, spoštovani minister, če ne verjamete v te stvari, ker to je neverjetno! In vsi skupaj, tudi vaši kolegi iz SDS, si zaslužijo boljše ljudi na položaju.
Example (Translation): Prove it! Everything else is harmful to you, and if it is harmful now, it will be harmful later, because that is karma, dear minister, if you do not believe in these things, because it is unbelievable! And all of you, including your colleagues from the SDS, deserve better people in office.
Notes/Rationale: Vaš kolega (Your colleague): Most often refers to political colleagues (allies from the same party, coalition, opposition, or ideological field), but can also refer to other (non-political) colleagues, though this is much less common.

Example: Če kdo v Državnem zboru oziroma v celotni državni politiki je bil komunist, je to vaš predsednik Janez Janša.
Example (Translation): If anyone in the National Assembly or in national politics as a whole was a communist, it was your president, Janez Janša.
Notes/Rationale: Vaš predsednik (Your president): Reference to either party president, Prime minister, or of the National Assembly. It is used to assign responsibility and blame to the speaker through their political leader.

Example: Ukrepajte! To je vaš resor. Ukrepajte ali pa enostavno ne pristajajte več na to in ne podpirajte te groze.
Example (Translation): Take action! This is your responsibility. Take action or simply refuse to accept this anymore and stop supporting this horror.
Notes/Rationale: Vaš resor (Your department): Similar function to your Ministry.
